problem_context: Scientific AI packets can sound plausible while failing to specify the concrete failure mode, intervention, mechanism, target endpoint, or falsification logic needed for review.
observed_failure_or_gap: Existing discovery-memory packets may preserve citations and provenance while still allowing unsupported mechanism jumps into reusable memory.
proposed_intervention_or_variable: Require a mechanism chain before memory promotion.
proposed_mechanism: The gate improves review quality because it forces the packet to connect an observed failure mode to an intervention via an explicit causal pathway and measurable endpoint.
target_endpoint: Reviewer agreement on whether a packet is ready for discovery-memory promotion.
validation_method: Compare curator agreement and unsupported-promotion rate before and after applying the mechanism-chain requirement to synthetic and real review packets.

source_map.title: Matter to Mechanism benchmark
source_map.claim_supported: Problem-to-hypothesis evaluation benefits from structured failure mode, intervention, mechanism, target property, and outcome fields.

source_map.title: HeurekaBench scientific agent benchmark
source_map.claim_supported: Scientific agents should be evaluated on end-to-end exploratory workflows grounded in datasets and reported findings.

source_status: Fresh public frontier scan using preprint and benchmark sources; no private data used.
missingness: No curator-labeled production corpus, no domain-expert review, and no CI wiring yet.
revision_reason: Prior gates emphasized provenance, judgment, privacy, and endpoints; this revision adds explicit mechanism-chain admission.
implementation_status: Schema, validator, fixtures, tests, and source-map README added under tools/mechanism_grounding_gate.
falsification_route: Reject or revise the gate if it fails to improve reviewer agreement or reduce unsupported discovery-memory promotion.
